title: uragoga muscosa
side_effects: Uragoga muscosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of saponins. Prolonged use can also lead to kidney damage and electrolyte imbalances, potentially resulting in seizures and cardiac arrhythmias. In extreme cases, improper use of Uragoga muscosa can cause respiratory failure, particularly in individuals with pre-existing respiratory conditions. Additionally, its high toxicity may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and even anaphylaxis in susceptible individuals.
